> *Your current cash is just below a full six-month emergency reserve.*

Arthur here. Based on **$5,400 in average monthly spending** over the 12 months from July 2025 through June 2026:

| Reserve level | Amount |
|---|---:|
| 3-month minimum | $16,200 |
| **Recommended target** | **$32,400** |
| Current checking and savings | $30,500 |
| Remaining gap | **$1,900** |

You currently have about **5.6 months of expenses** available. Because your household includes two adults and two young children, I recommend the six-month target rather than stopping at three months.

**Action:** Add another **$1,900** to emergency savings. At your observed **$900 monthly surplus**, you could reach that target in a little over two months. Keep the reserve in an insured, readily accessible savings account rather than investing it.

This estimate uses total spending, so a needs-only calculation could produce a somewhat lower target. Only one institution is currently visible, so the result may not include cash held elsewhere.
